_meta.description: humanize-en deterministic-cleanup pairs. ~85 pairs across 6 buckets. Applied by humanize_core.postprocess before the polish LLM so the LLM never has to 'delete' boilerplate — it just rewrites what's left.
_meta.license: MIT (this file); source attribution below is required but all sources are public-domain or MIT.
attribution: Plain English Campaign A-Z of alternative words (public domain, http://www.plainenglish.co.uk/files/alternative.pdf) — corporate_filler bucket
Strunk & White, Elements of Style, 1918 edition (public domain) — filler_opener bucket, empty_grand bucket
Liang et al. arXiv:2403.07183 'delve paper' — delve_class bucket, plain-English substitutes chosen by the humanize-en authors
ChatGPT safety-boilerplate list compiled from the HC3-en mining pass (scripts/mine_rule_candidates.py) — safety_disclaimer bucket
application_notes: Patterns are literal strings (no regex); matched as substrings, case-sensitive. Capitalisation variants that matter (e.g. 'In conclusion, ' at sentence start) are spelt out as separate pairs.
Buckets apply in the order declared by '_order'. Within each bucket, the loader re-sorts by len(old) descending so 'utilization' matches before 'utilize'.
Trailing spaces in 'old' are intentional — they let 'In conclusion, ' -> '' leave no double-space artefact when the filler sits between two words.
Empty 'new' means delete — chosen for meta-commentary and filler openers where the next clause reads fine without the hedge.

replacements._desc: Each bucket is a list of [old, new] pairs. Applied left-to-right; later pairs see output of earlier pairs.
safety_disclaimer: I don't have personal opinions, but
I do not have personal opinions, but
I do not have personal opinions.
It's important to note that I
It is important to note that I
meta_hedge: It is important to note that
I would like to point out that
I'd like to point out that
